mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of FEDFUNDS trend vs 20-day returns is below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating household income expectations and compresses aggregate demand, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when the trigger fires during backtest.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between retail sales and payroll momentum identifies the breadth of demand contraction; simultaneous negative growth in both proxies indicates collapsing aggregate demand and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint z-score of RSAFS and PAYEMS does not produce a lower mean 60-day return than either series alone (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Elevated inflation combined with a federal funds rate above 4% increases real discount rates and squeezes corporate margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are not significantly negative during periods when CPIAUCSL is in its top quartile and FEDFUNDS > 4%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals contracting manufacturing utilization and falling operating leverage, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered instances during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nominal retail sales reflects resilient consumer spending that supports corporate revenue expansion, predicting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Spearman correlation between RSAFS 3-month change and 60-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1,200 thousand units signals weakening construction activity and deteriorating household wealth effects, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in less than 55% of months when HOUST is below 1,200 during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: The joint acceleration of industrial production and real GDP growth indicates a broad-based supply expansion that lowers unit labor costs and expands profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of INDPRO and GDPC1 does not outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark on a risk-adjusted basis over a 3-year rolling window.
